title: kronos-semi input v1
description: Top-level input configuration for a kronos-semi simulation run. Describes the device geometry, materials, doping, contacts, physics models, solver parameters, and output controls. This schema is the contract between the UI form-builder and the engine; see docs/IMPROVEMENT_GUIDE.md §4 M11.

schema_version.description: Schema version in semver form. The engine refuses inputs whose major version does not match the engine's supported major. Minor/patch skew is allowed but logged. Current schema: 1.2.0.

name.title: Case name
name.description: Human-readable case name; used in output paths and plot titles.

description.title: Case description
description.description: Free-form description of the device and the numerical experiment. Optional.

dimension.title: Geometric dimension
dimension.description: Spatial dimension of the device mesh: 1 (line), 2 (area), or 3 (volume).

mesh.title: Mesh specification
mesh.description: How to construct or load the finite-element mesh, and how to tag regions and facets for boundary conditions. Either a builtin box mesh or an external gmsh/xdmf file.

oneOf.title: External mesh file
oneOf.description: Load a mesh from a gmsh .msh or XDMF file on disk. Physical groups in the file supply region and facet tags.

source.description: Selector: use an external mesh file instead of a builtin mesh.

path.description: Path to the mesh file, resolved relative to the input JSON file's directory.

format.description: Mesh file format. gmsh .msh files carry physical groups directly; XDMF is currently not implemented.

oneOf.title: Builtin box mesh
oneOf.description: Generate a structured axis-aligned interval/rectangle/box mesh directly, with regions and facets tagged by JSON-described boxes and planes.

source.description: Selector: generate a builtin structured mesh rather than loading one from disk.

extents.description: Bounds per dimension in meters, [[xmin, xmax], [ymin, ymax], ...]. Length must equal dimension.

resolution.description: Number of cells per dimension. Length must equal dimension.

regions_by_box.description: List of axis-aligned sub-boxes that tag mesh cells by region. Required when the device has more than one material region.

items.title: Region box
items.description: Axis-aligned sub-box used to tag all mesh cells whose centroid lies inside it with the named region tag.

name.description: Region name; must match a key in the top-level `regions` map.

tag.description: Integer cell tag assigned to cells in this box; used downstream by dx(subdomain_id=tag) integrals.

facets_by_plane.description: List of axis-aligned planes used to tag boundary facets for Dirichlet BCs and current-evaluation surfaces.

items.title: Facet plane
items.description: Axis-aligned plane whose facets carry the given tag, used to apply boundary conditions by symbolic contact name.

name.description: Facet name; referenced by `contacts[*].facet` as a string.

tag.description: Integer facet tag for boundary integrals.

axis.description: Coordinate axis orthogonal to the plane (0 = x, 1 = y, 2 = z).

value.description: Coordinate value along `axis` at which the plane lives, in meters.

regions.title: Region material map
regions.description: Region name -> material and role. Keys must match the names in mesh.regions_by_box (for builtin meshes) or the physical-group names in the gmsh file.

additionalProperties.title: Region definition
additionalProperties.description: Per-region material assignment and role (semiconductor or insulator). The material key must exist in the engine's material database.

material.description: Material name from the engine material database (e.g. Si, SiO2, Ge, GaAs).

tag.description: Optional explicit cell tag; usually inferred from mesh.regions_by_box.

role.description: Material role: `semiconductor` regions carry carriers and space charge; `insulator` regions carry only the dielectric Poisson term.

doping.title: Doping profiles
doping.description: List of doping profile specifications. Each entry targets one region and one profile kind (uniform / step / gaussian). Densities are in cm^-3 (device-physics convention) and are converted to m^-3 internally.

items.title: Doping entry
items.description: One doping profile applied to one named region.

region.description: Name of the region this profile is applied to; must be a key of the top-level `regions` map.

oneOf.title: Uniform doping
oneOf.description: Spatially constant donor and acceptor concentrations throughout the region. Densities in cm^-3.

N_D.description: Donor concentration, cm^-3. Zero means no donors.

N_A.description: Acceptor concentration, cm^-3. Zero means no acceptors.

oneOf.title: Step doping
oneOf.description: Piecewise-constant profile along one coordinate axis, with distinct donor/acceptor densities on each side of a metallurgical junction.

type.description: Selector for a one-dimensional step profile.

axis.description: Coordinate axis along which the step is located (0 = x, 1 = y, 2 = z).

location.description: Junction position along `axis`, in meters. Cells with coordinate < location are on the left side.

N_D_left.description: Donor concentration on the left side, cm^-3.

N_A_left.description: Acceptor concentration on the left side, cm^-3.

N_D_right.description: Donor concentration on the right side, cm^-3.

N_A_right.description: Acceptor concentration on the right side, cm^-3.

oneOf.title: Gaussian doping
oneOf.description: Anisotropic Gaussian bump (donor or acceptor) added on top of a background uniform doping. Useful for implant-style junctions.

center.description: Gaussian peak center coordinates, in meters; length equals dimension.

sigma.description: Per-axis Gaussian standard deviations, in meters; length equals dimension.

dopant.description: Whether the Gaussian adds to the donor or the acceptor profile.

background_N_D.description: Uniform donor background added everywhere in the region, cm^-3. Defaults to 0.

background_N_A.description: Uniform acceptor background added everywhere in the region, cm^-3. Defaults to 0.

contacts.title: Boundary contacts
contacts.description: List of contact boundary conditions (ohmic, ideal gate, or insulating). Each entry names a facet and the BC type to apply there.

items.title: Contact entry
items.description: One contact boundary condition. Ohmic contacts pin the potential and quasi-Fermi levels; gate contacts pin psi with a workfunction offset; insulating contacts contribute no Dirichlet row.

name.description: Human-readable contact name; used in IV output filenames and bias-sweep logs.

facet.description: Facet reference: either a symbolic name from mesh.facets_by_plane (string) or an integer facet tag from a gmsh physical group.

type.description: Contact type. `ohmic` enforces equilibrium majority-carrier concentrations and sets quasi-Fermi potentials to the applied voltage; `gate` is a Dirichlet BC on psi only with a workfunction offset; `insulating` is a no-flux Neumann BC.

voltage.description: Applied DC bias on this contact, in volts. Used when there is no `voltage_sweep` or as the starting point of one.

voltage_sweep.title: Voltage sweep
voltage_sweep.description: Continuation ramp on this contact. start -> stop in increments of at most `step`. When a single sweep crosses zero, the engine splits it into two unipolar legs.

start.description: Starting voltage for the ramp, in volts.

stop.description: Ending voltage for the ramp, in volts. May be less than `start` for reverse sweeps.

step.description: Initial voltage step size, in volts. The adaptive controller may shrink or grow this during the sweep.

workfunction.description: Gate metal workfunction difference phi_ms relative to the semiconductor intrinsic level, in volts. Only meaningful for `gate` contacts.

oxide_region.description: For gate contacts on multi-region meshes, the name of the insulator region separating the gate from the semiconductor.

physics.title: Physics model selections
physics.description: Temperature, statistics, recombination model, and mobility model. Every field has a sensible default; omit the block to accept defaults.

temperature.description: Device temperature in kelvin. Affects the thermal voltage V_t and all temperature-dependent material parameters.

recombination.title: Recombination model
recombination.description: Net recombination model selection. Currently SRH is implemented; Auger is an unimplemented flag for forward-compatibility.

E_t.description: Trap level measured from the intrinsic level, eV. Zero for a mid-gap trap.

auger.description: Placeholder Auger-recombination flag; not yet implemented in the engine.

mobility.title: Mobility model
mobility.description: Carrier mobility model. Currently only constant mobilities are supported; field-dependent models are scheduled for M16.

statistics.description: Carrier statistics model. Boltzmann is the only supported option today; Fermi-Dirac is scheduled for M16.

solver.title: Solver configuration
solver.description: Newton solver tolerances, linear-solver backend, continuation controls, and top-level solver type dispatch.

type.description: Which runner to dispatch. `equilibrium` solves Poisson at zero bias; `drift_diffusion` solves the coupled system at a single bias point; `bias_sweep` ramps a contact voltage; `mos_cv` walks a gate sweep and integrates gate charge; `transient` runs BDF1/BDF2 time integration; `ac_sweep` (M14) runs small-signal AC analysis around a DC operating point; `newton_block` is a legacy alias.

dc_bias.title: DC operating point for AC sweep
dc_bias.description: DC operating point around which the small-signal AC linearisation is performed. Required when solver.type is `ac_sweep`. The named contact is also the contact whose terminal admittance is reported.

contact.description: Name of the contact at which the DC bias is applied and around which the AC perturbation is computed. Must match a contact in the `contacts` list.

voltage.description: DC bias voltage at `contact`, in volts. The AC sweep linearises around this point.

ac.title: AC small-signal sweep specification
ac.description: AC perturbation amplitude and frequency sweep. Required when solver.type is `ac_sweep`. Admittance is reported per unit perturbation voltage; the amplitude is informational only because the response is linearised.

contact.description: Contact at which the AC perturbation is applied. Typically equal to `dc_bias.contact`.

amplitude.description: AC perturbation amplitude, V. Reported back in the result meta; the small-signal response itself is linear and is normalised to a per-unit-volt sensitivity inside the runner.

frequencies.title: AC frequency sweep specification
frequencies.description: Frequency points at which to evaluate Y(omega). Two forms: explicit `list`, or generative `logspace`/`linspace`.

oneOf.title: Explicit frequency list
oneOf.description: Use a fully specified array of frequencies (Hz). The runner evaluates Y(omega) at each entry verbatim; useful for benchmarks and for replicating a measurement instrument's preset frequency list.

values.description: Frequencies in Hz. f=0 is permitted (DC limit; capacitance reported as 0).

oneOf.title: Logarithmic frequency sweep
oneOf.description: Generate `n_points` log-spaced frequencies between `start` and `stop` (both Hz, both strictly positive). Standard for AC characterisation since admittance varies over many decades of frequency.

oneOf.title: Linear frequency sweep
oneOf.description: Generate `n_points` linearly spaced frequencies between `start` (Hz, may be 0) and `stop` (Hz, strictly positive). Useful for narrow-band sweeps where the response shape is known to be smooth.

n_points.description: Number of linearly spaced frequency points.

t_end.description: Simulation end time in seconds. Only used when solver.type is `transient`.

dt.description: Fixed time step size in seconds. Only used when solver.type is `transient`. Adaptive stepping is not supported in M13.

order.description: BDF time integration order. 1 = backward Euler (BDF1); 2 = BDF2. Only used when solver.type is `transient`.

max_steps.description: Safety cap on the number of time steps for the transient runner.

output_every.description: Write field snapshots every N transient time steps. Only used when solver.type is `transient`.

max_iterations.description: Maximum SNES iterations per Newton solve.

atol.description: Absolute residual tolerance for SNES convergence.

rtol.description: Relative residual tolerance for SNES convergence.

damping.description: Newton damping factor; 1.0 means no damping.

linear_solver.title: Linear solver options
linear_solver.description: PETSc KSP/PC options passed through to the inner linear solve at each Newton step. Defaults configure a direct MUMPS LU factorisation, which is bit-reproducible and suitable up to ~200k DOFs.

ksp_type.description: PETSc KSP type (e.g. preonly, gmres, cg).

pc_type.description: PETSc PC type (e.g. lu, hypre, amgx).

snes.title: PETSc SNES tolerance overrides for the bias_sweep DD block
snes.description: Per-config overrides for the SNES nonlinear solver tolerances inside the coupled drift-diffusion block. Defaults match docs/adr/0008-snes-tolerances.md (rtol=1e-10, atol=1e-7, stol=1e-14, max_it=100). Single-region 1D pn_1d_bias{,_reverse} benchmarks override these to machine-tight values to satisfy the 5%/15% current-continuity gates.

max_it.description: Maximum SNES iterations per Newton solve in the bias_sweep DD block.

continuation.title: Bias continuation controller
continuation.description: Adaptive step-size controller for bias sweeps. Halves the step on SNES failure and grows it after consecutive easy solves.

steps.description: Number of target sweep points; only used by the non-adaptive legacy path.

adaptive.description: Whether to enable adaptive step-size control. Disable for bit-reproducibility tests only.

min_step.description: Lower clamp on the continuation step, in volts. Halving is aborted once step < min_step.

max_halvings.description: Maximum consecutive step halvings before the sweep is abandoned.

max_step.description: Upper clamp on the continuation step, in volts.

easy_iter_threshold.description: SNES converging in strictly fewer than this many iterations counts as easy.

grow_factor.description: Multiplier on the continuation step after easy_iter_threshold consecutive easy solves.

sweep.title: Legacy fixed-point sweep
sweep.description: Legacy static voltage-sweep block. Retained for backwards compatibility; new code should prefer `contacts[*].voltage_sweep`.

contact.description: Name of the contact whose voltage is swept.

values.description: Explicit list of voltage values to solve at, in volts.

output.title: Output controls
output.description: Where to write results and which fields to include. Governs the on-disk artifact produced by `semi-run` and the HTTP server's run directory.

directory.description: Output directory (created if missing), resolved relative to the current working directory.

fields.description: Names of scalar fields to include in the artifact (e.g. potential, n, p, phi_n, phi_p).

write_xdmf.description: Whether to write XDMF/BP field files alongside the CSV IV tables.

write_iv.description: Whether to write IV CSV tables for bias-swept contacts.
